missing or empty\n\t// - ErrTypeFormat (format): Value format is invalid (e.g., email, UUID pattern)\n\t// - ErrTypeRange (range): Value is outside acceptable numeric range (min/max)\n\t// - ErrTypeLength (length): String length or collection size is invalid\n\t// - ErrTypeType (type): Value type is incorrect (e.g., string when int expected)\n\t// - ErrTypeValue (value): Value is invalid for domain-specific reasons\n\t// - ErrTypeDuplicate (duplicate): Duplicate value detected\n\t// - ErrTypeConflict (conflict): Conflict with existing values or constraints\n\t// - ErrTypeUnknown (unknown): Unknown error type (default/fallback)\n\n\terrorTypes := []ErrorType{\n\t\tErrTypeRequired,\n\t\tErrTypeFormat,\n\t\tErrTypeRange,\n\t\tErrTypeLength,\n\t\tErrTypeType,\n\t\tErrTypeValue,\n\t\tErrTypeDuplicate,\n\t\tErrTypeConflict,\n\t\tErrTypeUnknown,\n\t}\n\n\tfor {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_e259d660d00041cabb751586","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage validate\n2\t\n3\t/*\n4\tErrorType enum - Common validation error type categories\n5\t\n6\tThis file defines the ErrorType enum, which represents fundamental validation\n7\terror categories that can be used across different validation contexts. Unlike\n8\tValidationErrorType which is specific to HTTP/API validation, ErrorType provides\n9\tgeneric error type classifications for any validation scenario.\n10\t\n11\tCommon ErrorType values include:\n12\t  - ErrTypeRequired: Required field is missing or empty\n13\t  - ErrTypeFormat: Value format is invalid (e.g., email, UUID pattern)\n14\t  - ErrTypeRange: Value is outside acceptable range (min/max)\n15\t  - ErrTypeLength: String length or collection size is invalid\n16\t  - ErrTypeType: Value type is incorrect (e.g., string when int expected)\n17\t  - ErrTypeValue: Value is invalid for other reasons\n18\t  - ErrTypeDuplicate: Duplicate value detected\n19\t  - ErrTypeConflict: Conflict with existing values or constraints\n20\t  - ErrTypeUnknown: Unknown error type (default/fallback)\n21\t*/\n22\t\n23\timport (\n24\t\t\"fmt\"\n25\t\t\"strings\"\n26\t)\n27\t\n28\t// =============================================================================\n29\t// ERROR TYPE ENUM\n30\t// =============================================================================\n31\t\n32\t// ErrorType is a strongly-typed enum representing common validation error categories.\n33\t// It provides type safety and prevents typos when specifying error types.\n34\t//\n35\t// ErrorType represents fundamental validation failures that can occur in any\n36\t// validation context, unlike ValidationErrorType which is specific to HTTP/API validation.\n37\t//\n38\t// Use the ErrType* constants (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat) for type-safe\n39\t// error type specification. When you need to use these as strings, use\n40\t// string(ErrTypeRequired) or ErrTypeRequired.String().\n41\t//\n42\t// Usage example:\n43\t//\n44\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n45\t//\t    ErrorType: values.\n52\tconst (\n53\t\t// ErrTypeRequired indicates a required field is missing or empty.\n54\t\t// Use when a value must be present but is not provided.\n55\t\t// Example: \"email field is required\"\n56\t\tErrTypeRequired ErrorType = \"required\"\n57\t\n58\t\t// ErrTypeFormat indicates a value format is invalid.\n59\t\t// Use when a value doesn't match the expected format pattern.\n60\t\t// Examples: email format, UUID pattern, date format\n61\t\t// Example: \"email format is invalid (must contain @)\"\n62\t\tErrTypeFormat ErrorType = \"format\"\n63\t\n64\t\t// 120\"\n67\t\tErrTypeRange ErrorType = \"range\"\n68\t\n69\t\t// ErrTypeLength indicates string length or collection size is invalid.\n70\t\t// Use when a value is too short or too long.\n71\t\t// Example: \"password must be at least 8 characters\"\n72\t\tErrTypeLength ErrorType = \"length\"\n73\t\n74\t\t// ErrTypeType indicates value type is incorrect.\n75\t\t// Use when a value is not of the expected type.\n76\t\t// Example: \"expected number, got string\"\n77\t\tErrTypeType ErrorType = \"type\"\n78\t\n79\t\t// ErrTypeValue indicates a value is invalid for domain-specific ErrTypeUnknown\n236\t\t\tdefault:\n237\t\t\t\treturn ErrTypeUnknown\n238\t\t\t}\n239\t\t}\n240\t}\n241\t\n242\t// MustParseErrorType creates an ErrorType from a string.\n243\t// Panics if the string doesn't match any known type.\n244\t//\n245\t// This function is intended for use in initialization code where error types\n246\t// are expected to be constant and correct.\n247\t//\n248\t// Example =============================================================================\n350\t\n351\t// IsValidBasicErrorType checks if a string is a valid basic ErrorType enum value.\n352\t// This function works with the ErrorType enum (common validation errors),\n353\t// not the string-based ErrorType constants used for HTTP/API validation.\n354\t//\n355\t// Example usage:\n356\t//\n357\t//\tif validate\n\n/*\nErrorType enum - Common validation error type categories\n\nThis file defines the ErrorType enum, which represents fundamental validation\nerror categories that can be used across different validation contexts. Unlike\nValidationErrorType which is specific to HTTP/API validation, ErrorType provides\ngeneric error type classifications for any validation scenario.\n\nCommon ErrorType values include:\n  - ErrTypeRequired: Required field is missing or empty\n  - ErrTypeFormat: Value format is invalid (e.g., email, UUID pattern)\n  - ErrTypeRange: Value is outside acceptable range (min/max)\n  - ErrTypeLength: String length or collection size is invalid\n  - ErrTypeType: Value type is incorrect (e.g., string when int expected)\n  - ErrTypeValue: Value is invalid for other reasons\n  - ErrTypeDuplicate: Duplicate value detected\n  - ErrTypeConflict: Conflict with existing values or constraints\n  - ErrTypeUnknown: Unknown error type (default/fallback)\n*/\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE ENUM\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorType is a strongly-typed enum representing common validation error categories.\n// It provides type safety and prevents typos when specifying error types.\n//\n// ErrorType represents fundamental validation failures that can occur in any\n// validation context, unlike ValidationErrorType which is specific to HTTP/API validation.\n//\n// Use the ErrType* constants (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat) for type-safe\n// error type specification. When you need to use these as strings, use\n// string(ErrTypeRequired) or ErrTypeRequired.String().\n//\n// Usage example:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message:   \"Field 'email' is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\ntype ErrorType string\n\n// Common validation error type enum values.\nconst (\n\t// ErrTypeRequired indicates a required field is missing or empty.\n\t// Use when a value must be present but is not provided.\n\t// Example: \"email field is required\"\n\tErrTypeRequired ErrorType = \"required\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeFormat indicates a value format is invalid.\n\t// Use when a value doesn't match the expected format pattern.\n\t// Examples: email format, UUID pattern, date format\n\t// Example: \"email format is invalid (must contain @)\"\n\tErrTypeFormat ErrorType = \"format\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeRange indicates a value is outside acceptable numeric range.\n\t// Use when a numeric value is less than minimum or greater than maximum.\n\t// Example: \"age must be between 0 and 120\"\n\tErrTypeRange ErrorType = \"range\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeLength indicates string length or collection size is invalid.\n\t// Use when a value is too short or too long.\n\t// Example: \"password must be at least 8 characters\"\n\tErrTypeLength ErrorType = \"length\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeType indicates value type is incorrect.\n\t// Use when a value is not of the expected type.\n\t// Example: \"expected number, got string\"\n\tErrTypeType ErrorType = \"type\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeValue indicates a value is invalid for domain-specific reasons.\n\t// Use when a value is present and correctly formatted but still invalid.\n\t// Example: \"country code 'XX' is not recognized\"\n\tErrTypeValue ErrorType = \"value\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeDuplicate indicates a duplicate value was detected.\n\t// Use when a value must be unique but already exists.\n\t// Example: \"email 'user@example.com' already exists\"\n\tErrTypeDuplicate ErrorType = \"duplicate\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeConflict indicates a conflict with existing values or constraints.\n\t// Use when a value conflicts with business logic or other values.\n\t// Example: \"start date cannot be after end date\"\n\tErrTypeConflict ErrorType = \"conflict\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeUnknown indicates an unknown error type.\n\t// This is the default/fallback for unrecognized error types.\n\tErrTypeUnknown ErrorType = \"unknown\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE METHODS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// String returns the string representation of the ErrorType.\n// This implements the Stringer interface.\nfunc (et ErrorType) String() string {\n\treturn string(et)\n}\n\n// IsValid returns true if this is a valid ErrorType constant.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsValid() bool {\n\tswitch et {\n\tcase ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat, ErrTypeRange, ErrTypeLength,\n\t\tErrTypeType, ErrTypeValue, ErrTypeDuplicate, ErrTypeConflict,\n\t\tErrTypeUnknown:\n\t\treturn true\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n}\n\n// Description returns a human-readable description of this error type.\n// Returns \"Unknown error type\" if the type is not recognized.\nfunc (et ErrorType) Description() string {\n\tswitch et \"Value is invalid\"\n\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n\t\treturn \"Duplicate value detected\"\n\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n\t\treturn \"Conflict with existing values or constraints\"\n\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown error type\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown error type\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsRequired returns true if this is ErrTypeRequired.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsRequired() doesn't match any known type.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrorType := ErrorTypeFromString(\"required\")\n//\t// Returns: ErrTypeRequired\n//\n//\terrorType := This function is intended for use in initialization code where error types\n// are expected to be constant and correct.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrorType := {\n270\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType.IsValid() = %v, want %v\", tt.errorType.IsValid(), tt.expectValid)\n271\t\t\t\t}\n272\t\n273\t\t\t\t// Test Description method\n274\t\t\t\tif tt.errorType.Description() != tt.description {\n275\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType.Description() = %v, want %v\", tt.errorType.Description(), tt.description)\n276\t\t\t\t}\n277\t\n278\t\t\t\t// Test String method\n279\t\t\t\tif tt.errorType.String() != string(tt.errorType) {\n280\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType.String() = %v, want %v\", tt.errorType.String(), string(tt.errorType))\n281\t\t\t\t}\n282\t\n283\t\t\t\t// Test that FormatErrorWithType uses the ErrorType correctly\n284\t\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorWithType(tt.errorType, \"Test message\", \"field\")\n285\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, tt.errorType.String()) {\n286\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatErrorWithType result should contain error type string, got: %v\", =============================================================================\n418\t\n419\tfunc TestFormatError_BackwardCompatibility(t *testing.T) {\n420\t\t// Test that string-based =============================================================================\n626\t// INVALID ERROR TYPE TESTS\n627\t// =============================================================================\n628\t\n629\tfunc TestFormatError_InvalidErrorType(t *testing.T) {\n630\t\t// Test FormatError with invalid/unknown string \"mixed case error type (case-insensitive match, not tracked as invalid)\",\n667\t\t\t\terrorType: \"Required\",\n668\t\t\t\tmessage:   \"Field is required\",\n669\t\t\t\tfieldName: matching\n672\t\t\t},\n673\t\t\t{\n674\t\t\t\tname:      \"numeric error type\",\n675\t\t\t\terrorType: \"123\",\n676\t\t\t\tmessage:   \"Numeric error type\",\n677\t\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n678\t\t\t\twant:      \"[123] Numeric error type\",\n679\t\t\t\twantTracked: true,\n680\t\t\t},\n681\t\t}\n682\t\n683\t\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n684\t\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n685\t\t\t\t// Reset tracking before each test\n686\t\t\t\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n687\t\n688\t\t\t\t// Clear any existing tracking\n689\t\t\t\tinitialCount := InvalidErrorTypeCount()\n690\t\t\t\tif initialCount > 0 {\n691\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Warning: %d invalid error types already tracked\", initialCount)\n692\t\t\t\t}\n693\t\n694\t\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorString(tt.errorType, tt.message, tt.fieldName)\n695\t\n696\t\t\t\tif result != tt.want {\n697\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatError() = %q, want %q\", result, tt.want)\n698\t\t\t\t}\n699\t\n700\t\t\t\t// Verify tracking behavior matches expectations\n701\t\t\t\tinvalidTypes := GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n702\t\t\t\twasTracked := false\n703\t\t\t\tif count, found := invalidTypes[tt.errorType]; found && count > 0 {\n704\t\t\t\t\twasTracked = true\n705\t\t\t\t}\n706\t\n707\t\t\t\tif tt.wantTracked && !wasTracked {\n708\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error type %q should have been tracked as invalid\", tt.errorType)\n709\t\t\t\t}\n710\t\t\t\tif !tt.wantTracked && wasTracked && tt.errorType != \"\" && tt.errorType != \"unknown\" {\n711\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error type %q should NOT have been tracked as invalid\", tt.errorType)\n712\t\t\t\t}\n713\t\t\t})\n714\t\t}\n715\t}\n716\t\n717\tfunc TestFormatError_EmptyAndWhitespaceHandling(t *testing.T) {\n718\t\t// =============================================================================\n1139\t\n1140\tfunc contains(s, substr string) bool {\n1141\t\treturn strings.Contains(s, substr)\n1142\t}\n1143\t\n1144\tfunc hasPrefix(s, prefix string) bool {\n1145\t\treturn strings.HasPrefix(s, prefix)\n1146\t}\n1147\t\n1148\tfunc isWhitespace(s string) bool {\n1149\t\treturn strings.TrimSpace(s) == \"\" && s != \"\"\n1150\t}\n1151\t\n1152\t// =============================================================================\n1153\t// FORMATERROR STRING VALIDATION COMPREHENSIVE {\n1615\t\t// Comprehensive test of the error type tracking mechanism\n1616\t\tt.Run(\"tracking mechanism works correctly\", func(t *testing.T) {\n1617\t\t\t// Reset tracking\n1618\t\t\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n1619\t\n1620\t\t\t// Call FormatErrorString with multiple invalid types\n1621\t\t\tFormatErrorString(\"invalid_type_1\", \"message\", Duplicate\n1624\t\t\tFormatErrorString(\"custom_error\", \"message\", \"field4\")\n1625\t\n1626\t\t\tinvalidTypes := GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n1627\t\n1628\t\t\t// Verify counts\n1629\t\t\texpectedCounts := map[string]int{\n1630\t\t\t\t\"invalid_type_1\": 2,\n1631\t\t\t\t\"invalid_type_2\": 1,\n1632\t\t\t\t\"custom_error\":   1,\n1633\t\t\t}\n1634\t\n1635\t\t\tfor count, expectedCount)\n1638\t\t\t\t}\n1639\t\t\t}\n1640\t\n1641\t\t\t// Total unique invalid types\n1642\t\t\tif count := InvalidErrorTypeCount(); count != 3 {\n1643\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"InvalidErrorTypeCount() = %d, want 3\", count)\n1644\t\t\t}\n1645\t\t})\n1646\t\n1647\t\tt.Run(\"valid error types are not tracked\", func(t *testing.T) {\n1648\t\t\t// Reset tracking\n1649\t\t\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n1650\t\n1651\t\t\t// Use all valid error types\n1652\t\t\tvalidTypes := []string{\n1653\t\t\t\t\"required\", \"format\", \"range\", \"length\", TestFormatError_BackwardCompatibility(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test that string-based FormatErrorString produces consistent results\n\t// with ErrorType-based FormatError\n\tt.Run(\"string and ErrorType functions produce consistent results\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tmessage := \"Field is required\"\n\t\tfieldName := \"email\"\n\n\t\t// String-based function\n\t\tstringResult := FormatErrorString(\"required\", message, fieldName)\n\n\t\t// ErrorType-based function\n\t\ttypeResult := FormatError(ErrTypeRequired, message, fieldName)\n\n\t\tif stringResult != typeResult {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"String and ErrorType functions should produce same result:\\n  String: %v\\n  Type:   %v\", stringResult, typeResult)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"format error consistency\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tmessage := \"Invalid format\"\n\t\tfieldName := \"email\"\n\n\t\tstringResult := FormatErrorString(\"format\", message, fieldName)\n\t\ttypeResult := FormatError(ErrTypeFormat, message, fieldName)\n\n\t\tif stringResult != typeResult {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Format inconsistency:\\n  String: %v\\n  Type:   %v\", stringResult, typeResult)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"range error consistency\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tmessage := \"Value out of range\"\n\t\tfieldName := \"age\"\n\n\t\tstringResult := FormatErrorString(\"range\", message, fieldName)\n\t\ttypeResult := FormatError(ErrTypeRange, message, fieldName)\n\n\t\tif stringResult != typeResult {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Format inconsistency:\\n  String: %v\\n  Type:   %v\", stringResult, typeResult)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"type error consistency\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tmessage := \"Wrong type\"\n\t\tfieldName := \"count\"\n\n\t\tstringResult := FormatErrorString(\"type\", message, fieldName)\n\t\ttypeResult := FormatError(ErrTypeType, message, fieldName)\n\n\t\tif stringResult != typeResult {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Format inconsistency:\\n  String: %v\\n  Type:   %v\", stringResult, typeResult)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"length error consistency\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tmessage := \"Too short\"\n\t\tfieldName := \"password\"\n\n\t\tstringResult := FormatErrorString(\"length\", message, fieldName)\n\t\ttypeResult := FormatError(ErrTypeLength, message, fieldName)\n\n\t\tif stringResult != typeResult {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Format inconsistency:\\n  String: %v\\n  Type:   %v\", stringResult, typeResult)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"value error consistency\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tmessage := \"Invalid value\"\n\t\tfieldName := \"option\"\n\n\t\tstringResult := FormatErrorString(\"value\", message, fieldName)\n\t\ttypeResult := FormatError(ErrTypeValue, message, fieldName)\n\n\t\tif stringResult != typeResult {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Format inconsistency:\\n  String: %v\\n  Type:   %v\", stringResult, typeResult)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"duplicate error consistency\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tmessage := \"Already exists\"\n\t\tfieldName := \"email\"\n\n\t\tstringResult := FormatErrorString(\"duplicate\", message, fieldName)\n\t\ttypeResult := FormatError(ErrTypeDuplicate, message, fieldName)\n\n\t\tif stringResult != typeResult {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Format inconsistency:\\n  String: %v\\n  Type:   %v\", stringResult, typeResult)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"conflict error consistency\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tmessage := \"Conflicting values\"\n\t\tfieldName := \"dates\"\n\n\t\tstringResult := FormatErrorString(\"conflict\", message, fieldName)\n\t\ttypeResult := FormatError(ErrTypeConflict, message, fieldName)\n\n\t\tif stringResult != typeResult {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Format inconsistency:\\n  String: %v\\n  Type:   %v\", stringResult, typeResult)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatError_ConsistentClassification(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test that all ErrorType enum values are properly classified\n\tt.Run(\"all ErrorType values produce valid output\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tallTypes := []ErrorType{\n\t\t\tErrTypeRequired,\n\t\t\tErrTypeFormat,\n\t\t\tErrTypeRange,\n\t\t\tErrTypeLength,\n\t\t\tErrTypeType,\n\t\t\tErrTypeValue,\n\t\t\tErrTypeDuplicate,\n\t\t\tErrTypeConflict,\n\t\t\tErrTypeUnknown,\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, et := range allTypes {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorWithType(et, \"Test message\", \"field\")\n\n\t\t\t// Check that result contains error type\n\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, et.String()) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v should produce output containing %v, got %v\", et, et.String(), result)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Check that result contains field name\n\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \"field\") {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v should produce output containing field name, got %v\", et, result)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Check that result contains message\n\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \"Test message\") {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v should produce output containing message, got %v\", et, result)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"string-based error types are properly handled\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\terrorTypes := []string{\"required\", \"format\", \"range\", \"type\", \"unknown\"}\n\n\t\tfor _, etStr := range errorTypes {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorString(etStr, \"Test message\", \"field\")\n\n\t\t\t// Check that result contains error type\n\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, etStr) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error type %v should produce output containing error type, got %v\", etStr, result)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatError_ExistingCallsCompatibility(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test that existing FormatError calls continue to work correctly\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname      string\n\t\terrorType TYPE TESTS\n// =============================================================================\n\nfunc TestFormatError_InvalidErrorType(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test FormatError with invalid/unknown string error types\n\t// Invalid types should be tracked but still work (fallback behavior)\n\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname      string\n\t\terrorType string\n\t\tmessage   string\n\t\tfieldName string\n\t\twant      string\n\t\twantTracked bool // Whether the error type should be tracked as invalid\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"completely invalid error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"invalid_error_type\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Something went wrong\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"field\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[invalid_error_type] field: Something went wrong\",\n\t\t\twantTracked: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"custom error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"custom_validation\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Custom check failed\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[custom_validation] email: Custom check failed\",\n\t\t\twantTracked: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"typo in error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"require\", // typo for \"required\"\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"name\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[require] name: Field is required\",\n\t\t\twantTracked: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"mixed case error type (case-insensitive match, not tracked as invalid)\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"Required\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"age\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[Required] age: Field is required\",\n\t\t\twantTracked: false, // Should NOT be tracked as invalid due to case-insensitive matching\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"numeric error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"123\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Numeric error type\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[123] Numeric error type\",\n\t\t\twantTracked: true,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Reset tracking before each test\n\t\t\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n\n\t\t\t// Clear any existing tracking\n\t\t\tinitialCount := InvalidErrorTypeCount()\n\t\t\tif initialCount > 0 {\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Warning: %d invalid error types already tracked\", initialCount)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorString(tt.errorType, tt.message, tt.fieldName)\n\n\t\t\tif result != tt.want {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatError() = %q, want %q\", result, tt.want)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify tracking behavior matches expectations\n\t\t\tinvalidTypes := GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n\t\t\twasTracked := false\n\t\t\tif count, found := invalidTypes[tt.errorType]; found && count > 0 {\n\t\t\t\twasTracked = true\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif tt.wantTracked && !wasTracked {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error type %q should have been tracked as invalid\", tt.errorType)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif !tt.wantTracked && wasTracked && tt.errorType != \"\" && tt.errorType != \"unknown\" {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error type %q should NOT have been tracked as invalid\", tt.errorType)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc \"field1\")\n\tFormatErrorString(\"invalid_type_2\", \"message\", \"field2\")\n\tFormatErrorString(\"invalid_type_1\", \"message\", \"field3\") // duplicate\n\n\tinvalidTypes := GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n\n\t// Check counts\n\tif count := invalidTypes[\"invalid_type_1\"]; count != 2 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"invalid_type_1 count = %d, want 2\", count)\n\t}\n\tif count := invalidTypes[\"invalid_type_2\"]; count != 1 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"invalid_type_2 count = %d, want 1\", count)\n\t}\n\n\t// Total unique invalid types\n\tif count := InvalidErrorTypeCount(); count != 2 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"InvalidErrorTypeCount() = %d, want 2\", count)\n\t}\n\n\t// Reset and verify\n\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n\tif count := InvalidErrorTypeCount(); count != 0 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"After reset, InvalidErrorTypeCount() = %d, want 0\", count)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatError_ValidErrorTypesNotTracked(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test that valid ErrorType values are NOT tracked as invalid\n\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n\n\tvalidTypes := []string{\n\t\t\"required\", \"format\", \"range\", \"length\", \"type\",\n\t\t\"value\", \"duplicate\", \"conflict\", InvalidErrorTypeCount(); count != 0 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Valid error types should not be tracked, got %d tracked\", count)\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, errorType := range validTypes {\n\t\tif count, found := invalidTypes[errorType]; found {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Valid error type %q should not be tracked, but found count %d\", errorType, count)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatError_CaseInsensitiveErrorTypeMatching(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test that ErrorType matching is case-insensitive\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname      string\n\t\terrorType string\n\t\twantValid bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\"lowercase required\", \"required\", true},\n\t\t{\"uppercase REQUIRED\", \"REQUIRED\", true},\n\t\t{\"mixed case ReQuIrEd\", \"ReQuIrEd\", true},\n\t\t{\"lowercase format\", \"format\", true},\n\t\t{\"uppercase FORMAT\", \"FORMAT\", true},\n\t\t{\"mixed case FoRmAt\", \"FoRmAt\", true},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n\n\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorString(tt.errorType, \"test message\", \"field\")\n\n\t\t\t// Check if it was tracked as invalid\n\t\t\tinvalidTypes := GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n\t\t\twasTracked := false\n\t\t\tfor errorType := range invalidTypes {\n\t\t\t\tif errorType == tt.errorType {\n\t\t\t\t\twasTracked = true\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif tt.wantValid && wasTracked {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error type %q should be valid but was tracked as invalid\", tt.errorType)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// result)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatErrorWithType_EmptyFieldNameComprehensive(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Comprehensive tests for empty fieldName handling in FormatErrorWithType\n\ttests := \",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[length] Too long\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"tab-only fieldName\",\n\t\t\terrorType: ErrTypeType,\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Wrong type\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: fieldName doesn't appear in result when it's whitespace-only\n\t\t\tif isWhitespace(tt.fieldName) {\n\t\t\t\tif contains(result, tt.fieldName) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Whitespace-only fieldName should not appear in result: %q\", result)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatError_AllErrorTypeEnumValuesWork(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test that ALL ErrorType enum values produce valid output\n\tallTypes := []ErrorType{\n\t\tErrTypeRequired,\n\t\tErrTypeFormat,\n\t\tErrTypeRange,\n\t\tErrTypeLength,\n\t\tErrTypeType,\n\t\tErrTypeValue,\n\t\tErrTypeDuplicate,\n\t\tErrTypeConflict,\n\t\tErrTypeUnknown,\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, et := range allTypes {\n\t\tt.Run(et.String(), func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Test with all parameters\n\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorWithType(et, \"Test message\", \"field\")\n\n\t\t\t// Verify result is not empty\n\t\t\tif result == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v should produce non-empty output\", et)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify result contains error type\n\t\t\tif !contains(result, et.String()) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v output should contain error type string, got: %q\", et, result)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify result contains message\n\t\t\tif !contains(result, \"Test message\") {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v output should contain message, got: %q\", et, result)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify result contains field name\n\t\t\tif !contains(result, \"field\") {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v output should contain field name, got: %q\", et, result)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify structure starts with [error_type]\n\t\t\texpectedPrefix := \"[\" + et.String() + \"]\"\n\t\t\tif !hasPrefix(result, expectedPrefix) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v output should start with %v, got: %q\", et, expectedPrefix, result)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatError_ConsistencyWithTypeVariants(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test consistency between string-based FormatError and ErrorType-based FormatErrorWithType\n\ttestCases := []struct {\n\t\terrorTypeString string\n\t\terrorTypeEnum   ErrorType\n\t\tmessage         string\n\t\tfieldName       string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\"required\", ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\"},\n\t\t{\"format\", ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid format\", \"email\"},\n\t\t{\"range\", ErrTypeRange, \"Out of range\", \"age\"},\n\t\t{\"length\", ErrTypeLength, \"Too short\", \"password\"},\n\t\t{\"type\", ErrTypeType, \"Wrong type\", \"count\"},\n\t\t{\"value\", ErrTypeValue, \"Invalid value\", \"option\"},\n\t\t{\"duplicate\", ErrTypeDuplicate, \"Already exists\", \"email\"},\n\t\t{\"conflict\", ErrTypeConflict, \"Conflicting values\", \"dates\"},\n\t\t{\"unknown\", ErrTypeUnknown, \"Unknown error\", \"field\"},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tc := range testCases {\n\t\tt.Run(tc.errorTypeString, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Test with all parameters\n\t\t\tstringResult := FormatErrorString(tc.errorTypeString, tc.message, tc.fieldName)\n\t\t\tenumResult := FormatErrorWithType(tc.errorTypeEnum, tc.message, tc.fieldName)\n\n\t\t\tif stringResult != enumResult {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatError and FormatErrorWithType should produce identical results:\\n  String: %q\\n  Enum:   %q\", stringResult, enumResult)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Test without field name\n\t\t\tstringResultNoField := FormatErrorString(tc.errorTypeString, tc.message)\n\t\t\tenumResultNoField := FormatErrorWithType(tc.errorTypeEnum, tc.message, \"\")\n\n\t\t\tif stringResultNoField != enumResultNoField {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Results without field should match:\\n  String: %q\\n  Enum:   %q\", stringResultNoField, enumResultNoField)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\nfunc contains(s, substr string) bool {\n\treturn strings.Contains(s, substr)\n}\n\nfunc hasPrefix(s, prefix string) bool {\n\treturn strings.HasPrefix(s, prefix)\n}\n\nfunc isWhitespace(s string) bool {\n\treturn strings.TrimSpace(s) == \"\" && s != \"\"\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FORMATERROR STRING VALIDATION COMPREHENSIVE TESTS\n// =============================================================================\n\nfunc TestFormatError_StringValidation_ValidErrorTypes(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test FormatError with valid string error types that match ErrorType enum\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname      string\n\t\terrorType string\n\t\tmessage   string\n\t\tfieldName string\n\t\twant      string\n\t\twantValid bool // Whether this should be recognized as a valid ErrorType\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"valid required error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"required\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[required] email: Field is required\",\n\t\t\twantValid: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"valid format error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"format\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Invalid format\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[format] email: Invalid format\",\n\t\t\twantValid: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"valid range error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"range\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Out of range\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"age\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[range] age: Out of range\",\n\t\t\twantValid: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"valid length error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"length\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Too short\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"password\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[length] password: Too short\",\n\t\t\twantValid: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"valid type error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"type\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Wrong type\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"count\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[type] count: Wrong type\",\n\t\t\twantValid: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"valid value error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"value\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Invalid value\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"option\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[value] option: Invalid value\",\n\t\t\twantValid: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"valid duplicate error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"duplicate\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Already exists\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[duplicate] email: Already exists\",\n\t\t\twantValid: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"valid conflict error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"conflict\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Conflicting values\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"dates\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[conflict] dates: Conflicting values\",\n\t\t\twantValid: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"valid unknown error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"unknown\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Unknown error\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"field\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[unknown] field: Unknown error\",\n\t\t\twantValid: true,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Reset tracking before each test to ensure clean state\n\t\t\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n\n\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorString(tt.errorType, tt.message, tt.fieldName)\n\n\t\t\tif result != tt.want {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatError() = %q, want %q\", result, tt.want)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify that valid error types are NOT tracked as invalid\n\t\t\tinvalidTypes := GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n\t\t\tif tt.wantValid {\n\t\t\t\tif count, found := invalidTypes[tt.errorType]; found && count > 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Valid error type %q should NOT be tracked as invalid, but was tracked %d times\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\ttt.errorType, count)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatError_StringValidation_InvalidErrorTypes(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test FormatError with invalid string error types (fallback behavior)\n\t// Invalid types should be tracked for debugging but still work correctly\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname      string\n\t\terrorType string\n\t\tmessage   string\n\t\tfieldName string\n\t\twant      string\n\t\twantTracked bool // Whether this should be tracked as invalid\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"completely invalid error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"invalid_error_type\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Something went wrong\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"field\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[invalid_error_type] field: Something went wrong\",\n\t\t\twantTracked: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"custom validation error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"custom_validation\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Custom check failed\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[custom_validation] email: Custom check failed\",\n\t\t\twantTracked: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"typo in error type - require instead of required\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"require\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"name\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[require] name: Field is required\",\n\t\t\twantTracked: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"typo in error type - fomrat instead of format\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"fomrat\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Invalid format\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"data\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[fomrat] data: Invalid format\",\n\t\t\twantTracked: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"numeric error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"12345\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Numeric error type\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"count\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[12345] count: Numeric error type\",\n\t\t\twantTracked: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"special characters in error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"error-type-with-dashes\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Special error\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"field\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[error-type-with-dashes] field: Special error\",\n\t\t\twantTracked: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"error type with underscores\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"my_custom_error\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Custom error message\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"param\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[my_custom_error] param: Custom error message\",\n\t\t\twantTracked: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"mixed case typo - Requred instead of Required\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"Requred\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"age\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[Requred] age: Field is required\",\n\t\t\twantTracked: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"whitespace-only error type - tracked as invalid, defaults to 'error'\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"   \",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Test message\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"field\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[error] field: Test message\", // Whitespace trimmed to empty, defaults to \"error\"\n\t\t\twantTracked: true, // Whitespace-only is now tracked as invalid (user error, not intentional default)\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Reset tracking before each test\n\t\t\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n\n\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorString(tt.errorType, tt.message, tt.fieldName)\n\n\t\t\tif result != tt.want {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatError() = %q, want %q\", result, tt.want)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify tracking behavior\n\t\t\tinvalidTypes := GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n\t\t\twasTracked := false\n\t\t\tif count, found := invalidTypes[tt.errorType]; found && count > 0 {\n\t\t\t\twasTracked = true\n\t\t\t\tif count != 1 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Error type %q was tracked %d times\", tt.errorType, count)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif tt.wantTracked && !wasTracked {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error type %q should have been tracked as invalid\", tt.errorType)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif !tt.wantTracked && wasTracked {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error type %q should NOT have been tracked as invalid (count: %d)\",\n\t\t\t\t\ttt.errorType, invalidTypes[tt.errorType])\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatError_StringValidation_FallbackBehavior(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test fallback to default error type when invalid/empty type provided\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname      string\n\t\terrorType string\n\t\tmessage   string\n\t\tfieldName string\n\t\twant      string\n\t\tcheckFallback bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"empty error type falls back to 'error'\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Something went wrong\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[error] email: Something went wrong\",\n\t\t\tcheckFallback: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"empty error type without field\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Generic error\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[error] Generic error\",\n\t\t\tcheckFallback: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"empty error type with empty message falls back to default\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[error] email: email validation failed\",\n\t\t\tcheckFallback: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"empty error type, empty message, empty field - all fallbacks\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[error] (no message provided)\",\n\t\t\tcheckFallback: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"invalid error type does NOT fall back (backward compatibility)\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"invalid_type\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Error message\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"field\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[invalid_type] field: Error message\",\n\t\t\tcheckFallback: false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"custom error type does NOT fall back (backward compatibility)\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"my_custom_error\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Custom error\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"data\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[my_custom_error] data: Custom error\",\n\t\t\tcheckFallback: false,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Reset tracking to ensure clean state\n\t\t\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n\n\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorString(tt.errorType, tt.message, tt.fieldName)\n\n\t\t\tif result != tt.want {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatError() = %q, want %q\", result, tt.want)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Check that fallback to \"error\" only happens for empty errorType\n\t\t\tif tt.checkFallback {\n\t\t\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(result, \"[error]\") {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected fallback to '[error]', got: %q\", result)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Empty error type should NOT be tracked as invalid\n\t\t\t\tinvalidTypes := GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n\t\t\t\tif count, found := invalidTypes[\"\"]; found && count > 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Empty error type should NOT be tracked as invalid, count: %d\", count)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatError_StringValidation_CaseSensitivity(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test case sensitivity of error type strings\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname         string\n\t\terrorType    string\n\t\tmessage      string\n\t\tfieldName    string\n\t\twant         string\n\t\tshouldBeValid bool // Should match a valid ErrorType (case-insensitive)\n\t\tshouldTrack  bool  // Should be tracked as invalid\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"lowercase required\",\n\t\t\terrorType:    \"required\",\n\t\t\tmessage:      \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:    \"email\",\n\t\t\twant:         \"[required] email: Field is required\",\n\t\t\tshouldBeValid: true,\n\t\t\tshouldTrack:  false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"uppercase REQUIRED\",\n\t\t\terrorType:    \"REQUIRED\",\n\t\t\tmessage:      \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:    \"email\",\n\t\t\twant:         \"[REQUIRED] email: Field is required\",\n\t\t\tshouldBeValid: true, // Should match via case-insensitive lookup\n\t\t\tshouldTrack:  false, // Should NOT be tracked - ErrorTypeFromString recognizes it\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"mixed case ReQuIrEd\",\n\t\t\terrorType:    \"ReQuIrEd\",\n\t\t\tmessage:      \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:    \"name\",\n\t\t\twant:         \"[ReQuIrEd] name: Field is required\",\n\t\t\tshouldBeValid: true, // Should match via case-insensitive lookup\n\t\t\tshouldTrack:  false, // Should NOT be tracked - ErrorTypeFromString recognizes it\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"lowercase format\",\n\t\t\terrorType:    \"format\",\n\t\t\tmessage:      \"Invalid format\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:    \"data\",\n\t\t\twant:         \"[format] data: Invalid format\",\n\t\t\tshouldBeValid: true,\n\t\t\tshouldTrack:  false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"uppercase FORMAT\",\n\t\t\terrorType:    \"FORMAT\",\n\t\t\tmessage:      \"Invalid format\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:    \"email\",\n\t\t\twant:         \"[FORMAT] email: Invalid format\",\n\t\t\tshouldBeValid: true, // Should match via case-insensitive lookup\n\t\t\tshouldTrack:  false, // Should NOT be tracked - ErrorTypeFromString recognizes it\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"mixed case RaNgE\",\n\t\t\terrorType:    \"RaNgE\",\n\t\t\tmessage:      \"Out of range\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:    \"age\",\n\t\t\twant:         \"[RaNgE] age: Out of range\",\n\t\t\tshouldBeValid: true, // Should match via case-insensitive lookup\n\t\t\tshouldTrack:  false, // Should NOT be tracked - ErrorTypeFromString recognizes it\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"Title Case Required\",\n\t\t\terrorType:    \"Required\",\n\t\t\tmessage:      \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:    \"field\",\n\t\t\twant:         \"[Required] field: Field is required\",\n\t\t\tshouldBeValid: true, // Should match via case-insensitive lookup\n\t\t\tshouldTrack:  false, // Should NOT be tracked - ErrorTypeFromString recognizes it\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"mixed case typo - ReQuRed (typo, not just case)\",\n\t\t\terrorType:    \"ReQuRed\",\n\t\t\tmessage:      \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:    \"test\",\n\t\t\twant:         \"[ReQuRed] test: Field is required\",\n\t\t\tshouldBeValid: false, // Typo, not valid\n\t\t\tshouldTrack:  true,  // Should be tracked as invalid\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"lowercase unknown\",\n\t\t\terrorType:    \"unknown\",\n\t\t\tmessage:      \"Unknown error\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:    \"field\",\n\t\t\twant:         \"[unknown] field: Unknown error\",\n\t\t\tshouldBeValid: true,\n\t\t\tshouldTrack:  false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"uppercase UNKNOWN\",\n\t\t\terrorType:    \"UNKNOWN\",\n\t\t\tmessage:      \"Unknown error\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:    \"data\",\n\t\t\twant:         \"[UNKNOWN] data: Unknown error\",\n\t\t\tshouldBeValid: true, // Should match via case-insensitive lookup\n\t\t\tshouldTrack:  false, // Should NOT be tracked - ErrorTypeFromString recognizes it\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Reset tracking before each test\n\t\t\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n\n\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorString(tt.errorType, tt.message, tt.fieldName)\n\n\t\t\tif result != tt.want {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatError() = %q, want %q\", result, tt.want)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify tracking behavior\n\t\t\tinvalidTypes := GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n\t\t\twasTracked := false\n\t\t\tif count, found := invalidTypes[tt.errorType]; found && count > 0 {\n\t\t\t\twasTracked = true\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif tt.shouldTrack && !wasTracked {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error type %q should have been tracked as invalid\", tt.errorType)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif !tt.shouldTrack && wasTracked {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error type %q should NOT have been tracked as invalid (count: %d)\",\n\t\t\t\t\ttt.errorType, invalidTypes[tt.errorType])\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify that valid error types match the ErrorType enum\n\t\t\tif tt.shouldBeValid {\n\t\t\t\t// Convert to ErrorType and check if it's valid\n\t\t\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(tt.errorType)\n\t\t\t\tif !et.IsValid() {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Special case: if the error type string is different case than enum,\n\t\t\t\t\t// ErrorTypeFromString should still return the correct enum value\n\t\t\t\t\tlowerType := strings.ToLower(tt.errorType)\n\t\t\t\t\tetFromLower := ErrorTypeFromString(lowerType)\n\t\t\t\t\tif !etFromLower.IsValid() {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error type %q should be valid via case-insensitive matching\", tt.errorType)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatError_StringValidation_ErrorTypeTrackingMechanism(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Comprehensive test of the error type tracking mechanism\n\tt.Run(\"tracking mechanism works correctly\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Reset tracking\n\t\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n\n\t\t// Call FormatErrorString with multiple invalid \"field3\") // Duplicate\n\t\tFormatErrorString(\"custom_error\", \"message\", \"field4\")\n\n\t\tinvalidTypes := GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n\n\t\t// Verify counts\n\t\texpectedCounts := map[string]int{\n\t\t\t\"invalid_type_1\": 2,\n\t\t\t\"invalid_type_2\": 1,\n\t\t\t\"custom_error\":   1,\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor errorType, expectedCount := range expectedCounts {\n\t\t\tif count := invalidTypes[errorType]; count != expectedCount {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error type %q count = %d, want %d\", errorType, count, expectedCount)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Total unique invalid types\n\t\tif count := InvalidErrorTypeCount(); count != 3 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"InvalidErrorTypeCount() = %d, want 3\", count)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"valid error types are not tracked\", func(t *testing.T) \"type\",\n\t\t\t\"value\", \"duplicate\", \"conflict\", \"unknown\",\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, errorType := range validTypes {\n\t\t\tFormatErrorString(errorType, \"test message\", \"field\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tinvalidTypes := GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n\t\tif count := InvalidErrorTypeCount(); count != 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Valid error types should not be tracked, got %d tracked\", count)\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Tracked types: %v\", invalidTypes)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"tracking reset works correctly\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Reset tracking\n\t\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n\n\t\t// Add some invalid types\n\t\tFormatErrorString(\"type1\", \"msg\", \"field\")\n\t\tFormatErrorString(\"type2\", \"msg\", \"field\")\n\n\t\tif count)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check specific types\n\t\tif count, found := invalidTypes[\"required\"]; found && count > 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Valid type 'required' should not be tracked\")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif count, found := invalidTypes[\"format\"]; found && count > 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Valid type 'format' should not be tracked\")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif count, found := invalidTypes[\"range\"]; found && count > 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Valid type 'range' should not be tracked\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Verify invalid types are tracked\n\t\tif count, found := invalidTypes[\"invalid_type_1\"]; !found || count != 1
